attacking_style: Attempts independent attacks on final climbs but lacks explosive acceleration to sustain separation from elite GC riders. Probing moves typically absorbed by stronger climbers within same selection.
team_tactics: Movistar employs multi-leader strategy giving him protected status, but lacks dedicated mountain train. Often isolated on decisive climbs when teammates also target GC or fade early.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained climbs suit him better than explosive steep ramps where younger Spanish rivals (Ayuso) drop him. Completely exposed in time trials with losses exceeding 1 minute repeatedly.
vulnerability: Time trials catastrophically undermine GC campaigns (1+ minute losses eliminating podium threat). Fades in final 300m of efforts showing declining sprint endurance. Dropped when top teams raise early tempo.
key_patterns: Time trial liability eliminates GC contention: 1+ minute losses observed multiple Vueltas (2022, 2023)
Attacks neutralized by stronger climbers: Evenepoel, Roglic groups consistently absorb his moves (4+ instances)
Declining finishing power: faded barriers final 300m Tirreno, caught by Ayuso on steep sections
Tactical cooperative rider: chased splits for podium, waited for Alaphilippe, bridged independently when needed
Form regression 2023: dropped early Lombardia, 2.5min TT loss Dauphine, leadout fade Tirreno
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows systematic decline across all disciplines - early Monument elimination, catastrophic TT regression, and sprint endurance fade after competitive 2022 Vuelta podium
